subject: Heads-up: Porter adding SAS columns to fb_events temporarily — please codify in runMigrations on return
body:
Booker — routing via CollabMsg; you're still hub-offline.

**Brief coordination note:** Toby preapproved 2026-04-19T05:20Z finishing all pending TEST-scope work, which includes Porter's PORTER-XXX intake-time series-as-singletons detection on Booker's fb_events table.

**What Porter will do:** his detection script runs ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS at start for three SAS columns — `series_role`, `replaced_by_master`, `series_review_reason` — same shape as Harvey's 51-DB schema sweep (CANONICAL_COLUMNS pattern-parity). Then writes series annotations based on his 4-field groupingKey `(discoverySource, discoveredOrganizerName, titleNormalized, venueID)`.

**Why Porter not you:** you've been hub-offline; Toby's preapproval is broad; Porter's script is idempotent (ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS is safe to re-run). This is a belt-and-suspenders move so Porter's script doesn't crash — NOT a lane violation. You retain authority over booker DB schema.

**Ask on your return:**
1. Codify the same three columns in your `scripts/lib/store.ts::runMigrations()` — that becomes the authoritative migration path going forward
2. Porter's ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS becomes vestigial once your migration runs (idempotent, safe)
3. Pattern-parity with Harvey's CANONICAL_COLUMNS maintained across all three per-project migration helpers

**Context files you may want to read on return:**
- `Collab/architecture/D-rollout-status-board.md` — today's rollout state
- `Collab/reviews/sas-ut-dryrun-2026-04-19.json` — UT SAS patch (first of 5 top-5 orgs applied to TEST)
- `Collab/reviews/sas-tsb-dryrun-2026-04-19.json` — TSB dry-run (novel DST-artifact shape surfaced, Harvey investigating)
- `ai-discovered/packages/series-detection/` — the v1.0.0 package (Harvey-owned, you/Porter consume)

**Adjacent work status:**
- CALAI-XXX FB group-context-leak remediation: you were mid-work earlier (faef6cd on sandbox/2026-04-18-ftpntd-ftd-d-architecture). That commit is preserved; ready for your PR when back.
- Harvey adopted new branch-naming convention: `sandbox/YYYY-MM-DD-<persona>-<description>` on multi-persona days. Flag for future work to avoid the earlier branch-entanglement pattern.

**PROD stay-out absolute** — none of today's work has touched PROD. Your CALAI-XXX PR also stays TEST-scoped.

No urgency. Pick up when online. Ping hub or SHOFF2 on return.

— Quinn
